description:
In secretx-tpm2/src/lib.rs lines 310-311, the put() method creates a redundant intermediate Zeroizing copy:

```rust
let owned = Zeroizing::new(bytes.to_vec());
let data = MaxNvBuffer::try_from(owned.as_slice().to_vec())...
```

The review prompt asks whether this fix actually helps, noting that MaxNvBuffer::try_from gets a non-Zeroizing Vec. Investigation shows that MaxNvBuffer internally wraps Zeroizing<Vec<u8>> (confirmed in tss-esapi 8.0.0-alpha.2 buffers.rs macro), so ALL copies are zeroized on drop. There is no unzeroized copy.

However, `owned` is completely redundant. `bytes` is already Zeroizing<Vec<u8>> (from value.into_bytes()). The code creates owned = Zeroizing::new(bytes.to_vec()), then immediately does owned.as_slice().to_vec() -- two unnecessary allocations.

Fix: replace both lines with `let data = MaxNvBuffer::try_from(bytes.to_vec())...` or even better `MaxNvBuffer::try_from(Zeroizing::into_inner(bytes))...` to avoid any extra copy. Also update the misleading comment on line 309 ("Wrap in Zeroizing so the copy is zeroed on drop") -- the MaxNvBuffer itself handles zeroization; the comment implies it does not.

Severity: low (code clarity + one wasted allocation, not a security bug).
title: put() Zeroizing: redundant copy and misleading comment
